title: Senate to Vote on New Homeland Security Leader
summary: The Senate is planning to vote to make Senator Markwayne Mullin the new Homeland Security secretary. He is likely to get the job because many senators support him. In London, police are looking into a fire that burned ambulances belonging to a Jewish group. They think it was a hate crime against Jewish people. No one was hurt, and the police are searching for three people. In Cuba, the electricity keeps going out, making life hard for people. They have trouble keeping food fresh and don't know when the power will come back on. These are three big news stories from around the world.

keywords.explanation: a group of lawmakers in the U.S. government who make decisions
a department that helps keep the country safe
when someone sets a fire on purpose to damage things
showing hate or unfair treatment toward Jewish people
when the electricity stops working, making everything dark

questions.question: What is the Senate voting on?
Who is likely to become the new Homeland Security secretary?
Why are police investigating the fire in London?
What problem is happening in Cuba?
How do blackouts affect people in Cuba?
options: They make it easier to cook

background_read: The U.S. Senate is part of Congress and helps choose leaders for government jobs. Homeland Security works on safety issues like borders and emergencies. Hate crimes are bad acts against people because of their race or religion. Blackouts happen when power systems fail, often due to old equipment or lack of fuel.
Article_Structure: WHO: The Senate, Senator Markwayne Mullin, police in London, people in Cuba
WHAT: Voting on a new Homeland Security secretary, investigating a fire as a hate crime, dealing with electricity blackouts
WHY: To choose a leader for safety, to stop hate crimes, to fix power problems

perspectives.description: Leaders are making decisions to keep countries safe and address problems.
People are affected by crimes and blackouts, needing help and solutions.
